0.original: Ideas are fruits of your thinking, but they've got to be harnessed and put to work to have value.
0.b1: Ideas are the result of your thinking, but you must use them to make them valuable.
0.cn: 想法是你思考的果实，但必须加以利用并付诸实践才有价值。
0.ai_notes: 'harnessed': controlled and directed to make something useful. 'put to work': to start using something in a practical way.
0.remarks:
'harnessed': 被控制并引导以产生用途。
'put to work': 开始以实际方式使用某物。

1.original: Each year, an oak tree produces enough acorns to populate a good-sized forest.
1.b1: Every year, an oak tree makes enough acorns to grow a large forest.
1.cn: 每年，一棵橡树产生的橡子足以长成一片大森林。
1.ai_notes: 'acorns': the nut of an oak tree. 'populate': to fill a place or area.
1.remarks:
'acorns': 橡树的果实（橡子）。
'populate': 填充某个地方或区域。

2.original: Yet, from these bushels of seeds, perhaps only one or two acorns will become a tree.
2.b1: However, from all these seeds, maybe only one or two will become a tree.
2.cn: 然而，在这些成堆的种子中，也许只有一两颗能长成大树。
2.ai_notes: 'bushels': a large amount or quantity. 'seeds': the small parts of a plant that can grow into new plants.
2.remarks:
'bushels': 大量或多数量。
'seeds': 植物的种子，可生长成新植物。

3.original: The squirrels destroy most of them, and the hard ground beneath the tree doesn't give the few remaining seeds much chance for a start.
3.b1: Squirrels eat most of them, and the hard ground makes it difficult for the rest to grow.
3.cn: 松鼠吃掉了大部分，而树下坚硬的土地让剩下的种子很难生根发芽。
3.ai_notes: 'beneath': directly under something. 'give... a start': to provide the necessary conditions for something to begin growing.
3.remarks:
'beneath': 在某物正下方。
'give... a start': 为某事开始生长提供必要条件。

4.ai_notes: 'So it is with...': a formal way to say that a situation is the same for something else.
4.remarks: 'So it is with...': 一种正式表达方式，意为某种情况同样适用于其他事物。

5.cn: 很少有想法能结出果实。
5.ai_notes: 'bear fruit': an idiom meaning to produce successful results.
5.remarks: 'bear fruit': 习语，意为产生成功的成果。

6.ai_notes: 'perishable': something that can easily decay, spoil, or be lost if not taken care of.
6.remarks: 'perishable': 指如果不妥善保管就容易腐烂、变质或丢失的东西。

7.original: If we're not on guard, the squirrels, negative-thinking people, will destroy most of them.
7.b1: If we are not careful, negative people will destroy most of our ideas.
7.cn: 如果我们不加防范，那些消极的人就会摧毁我们的大部分想法。
7.ai_notes: 'on guard': alert and ready to protect against danger. 'negative-thinking people': people who focus on problems rather than solutions.
7.remarks:
'on guard': 警惕并准备好防范危险。
'negative-thinking people': 关注问题而非解决方案的人。

8.original: Ideas require special handling from the time they are born until they're transformed into practical ways for doing things better.
8.b1: Ideas need care from the moment they appear until they become useful tools.
8.cn: 想法从诞生到转化为改进工作的实用方法，都需要特别的呵护。
8.ai_notes: 'special handling': extra care or attention. 'transformed': changed completely into something else.
8.remarks:
'special handling': 特别的照顾或关注。
'transformed': 完全改变成其他事物。

9.original: Use these three ways to harness and develop your ideas.
9.b1: Use these three ways to manage and grow your ideas.
9.cn: 用这三种方法来掌控和发展你的想法。
9.ai_notes: 'harness': to capture and use a resource for a specific purpose.
9.remarks: 'harness': 捕获并利用资源以达到特定目的。

10.ai_notes: '1.': This is the start of a list of instructions.

11.b1: Do not let your ideas disappear.
11.ai_notes: 'escape': to get away or be lost before you can catch it.
11.remarks: 'escape': 在你抓住它之前溜走或丢失。

12.ai_notes: 'Write them down': to record information on paper so you do not forget it.
12.remarks: 'Write them down': 将信息记录在纸上以免忘记。

13.original: Every day lots of good ideas are born only to die quickly because they aren't nailed to paper.
13.b1: Many good ideas are born every day, but they die quickly because they are not written down.
13.cn: 每天都有许多好想法诞生，但因为没有记在纸上，很快就消失了。
13.ai_notes: 'nailed to paper': a metaphor meaning to write something down firmly so it cannot be lost.
13.remarks: 'nailed to paper': 比喻，意为将某事牢牢写在纸上以免丢失。

14.original: Memory is a weak slave when it comes to preserving and nurturing brand new ideas.
14.b1: Memory is not good enough to keep and develop new ideas.
14.cn: 在保存和培育新想法方面，记忆力是靠不住的。
14.ai_notes: 'weak slave': a metaphor suggesting that your memory is not reliable enough to be trusted.
14.remarks: 'weak slave': 比喻，暗示你的记忆力不可靠，不能完全信任。

15.original: Carry a notebook or some small cards with you.
15.b1: Carry a notebook or small cards with you.
15.cn: 随身携带一个笔记本或一些小卡片。
15.ai_notes: 'Carry... with you': to keep something on your person at all times.
15.remarks: 'Carry... with you': 随身携带某物。

16.original: When you get an idea, write it down.
16.b1: When you have an idea, write it down immediately.
16.cn: 当你有了想法，就把它写下来。
16.ai_notes: 'get an idea': to have a sudden thought or inspiration.
16.remarks: 'get an idea': 突然产生一个想法或灵感。

17.original: A friend who travels a lot keeps a clipboard beside him so that he can write down an idea the instant it occurs to him.
17.b1: A friend of mine keeps a clipboard nearby to write down ideas as soon as he thinks of them.
17.cn: 我的一位朋友随身带着写字板，以便在想法出现的瞬间就能记下来。
17.ai_notes: 'clipboard': a board with a clip at the top for holding papers. 'the instant': immediately at that exact moment.
17.remarks:
'clipboard': 顶部带有夹子的板子，用于固定纸张。
'the instant': 就在那一刻，立刻。

18.original: People with fertile creative minds know a good idea may sprout any time, any place.
18.b1: Creative people know that a good idea can happen anywhere at any time.
18.cn: 富有创造力的人知道，好想法随时随地都可能出现。
18.ai_notes: 'fertile creative minds': minds that are very productive and full of new ideas. 'sprout': to begin to grow or appear suddenly.
18.remarks:
'fertile creative minds': 极具生产力且充满新想法的头脑。
'sprout': 突然开始生长或出现。

19.original: Don't let ideas escape, else you destroy the fruits of your thinking.
19.b1: Do not let ideas escape, or you will lose the results of your thinking.
19.cn: 不要让想法溜走，否则你就是摧毁了自己思考的果实。
19.ai_notes: 'else': a short way of saying 'otherwise' or 'if you do not do this'.
19.remarks: 'else': 'otherwise'（否则）或 'if you do not do this'（如果你不这样做）的简写。

20.ai_notes: 'Fence them in': a metaphor meaning to keep your ideas contained and safe in one place.
20.remarks: 'Fence them in': 比喻，意为将你的想法限制并安全地保存在一个地方。

21.ai_notes: '2.': This marks the second step in the process.

22.b1: Next, look at your ideas again.
22.ai_notes: 'review': to look over something again to check its value.
22.remarks: 'review': 再次查看某物以检查其价值。

23.original: File these ideas in an active file.
23.b1: Keep your ideas in a special place.
23.cn: 把这些想法归档到一个活跃的文件夹里。
23.ai_notes: 'active file': a collection of notes that you check and use frequently.
23.remarks: 'active file': 你经常查看和使用的笔记集合。

24.original: The file can be an elaborate cabinet, or it can be a desk drawer.
24.b1: This place can be a big cabinet or just a desk drawer.
24.cn: 这个地方可以是一个精致的文件柜，也可以是一个抽屉。
24.remarks: 'elaborate': 复杂的、详细的或精致的。

25.ai_notes: 'will do': an expression meaning that something is sufficient or good enough.
25.remarks: 'will do': 表达某物足够好或可以胜任。

26.original: But build a file, and then examine your storehouse of ideas regularly.
26.b1: Create a file and check your collection of ideas often.
26.cn: 建立一个档案，并定期检查你的想法库。
26.ai_notes: 'storehouse': a place where things are kept for future use.
26.remarks: 'storehouse': 存放东西以备将来使用的地方。

27.original: As you go over your ideas, some may, for very good reasons, have no value at all.
27.b1: When you review your ideas, you might find some are not useful.
27.cn: 当你回顾这些想法时，有些可能因为各种原因变得毫无价值。
27.ai_notes: 'go over': to examine or look at something carefully.
27.remarks: 'go over': 仔细检查或查看某物。

28.ai_notes: 'Get rid of': to throw away or remove something you no longer need.
28.remarks: 'Get rid of': 扔掉或移除你不再需要的东西。

29.original: But so long as the idea has any promise, keep it.
29.b1: But if an idea still seems good, keep it.
29.cn: 但只要这个想法还有希望，就保留它。
29.ai_notes: 'promise': potential; a sign that something might be successful in the future.
29.remarks: 'promise': 潜力；预示某事未来可能成功的迹象。

30.ai_notes: '3.': This marks the third and final step in the process.
30.remarks: '3.': 这是过程中的第三步，也是最后一步。

31.ai_notes: 'Cultivate': to prepare and use land for crops; here, it means to help an idea develop.
31.remarks: 'Cultivate': 耕作土地；此处指帮助一个想法发展。

32.cn: 现在，让你的想法成长起来。
32.ai_notes: 'make your idea grow': to expand or add more detail to your initial thought.
32.remarks: 'make your idea grow': 扩展或为你的初步想法增加更多细节。

33.ai_notes: 'Think about it': to consider an idea deeply and carefully.
33.remarks: 'Think about it': 深入且仔细地考虑一个想法。

34.original: Tie the idea to related ideas.
34.b1: Connect the idea to other related ideas.
34.cn: 将该想法与相关想法联系起来。
34.ai_notes: 'Tie... to': to connect or link one thing with another.
34.remarks: 'Tie... to': 将一件事与另一件事联系起来。

35.original: Read anything you can find that is in any way akin to your idea.
35.b1: Read anything that is related to your idea.
35.cn: 阅读任何与你的想法相关的内容。
35.ai_notes: 'akin to': similar to or related to.

36.b1: Look at the idea from all sides.
36.cn: 从各个角度进行调查研究。
36.ai_notes: 'all angles': every possible perspective or point of view.
36.remarks: 'all angles': 每个可能的视角或观点。

37.original: Then, when the time is ripe, put it to work for yourself, your job, your future.
37.b1: Then, when the time is right, use it to improve your work and your future.
37.cn: 然后，当时机成熟时，将其付诸实践，造福你自己、你的工作和你的未来。
37.ai_notes: 'time is ripe': an idiom meaning the perfect moment for action has arrived.
37.remarks: 'time is ripe': 习语，意为采取行动的最佳时机已经到来。
